page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server configured with 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at 2.2 GHz, the instance features 20 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 640 KB of L1d cache, 5 MB of L2 cache, and 55 MB of L3 cache. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear trade-off between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds for large blocks, although the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput perform within the middle 50% range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m1-ultramem-40 represents a specialized hardware profile where resource density is heavily weighted toward memory capacity rather than raw CPU compute speed. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 24 GB per core makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded performance. Appropriate workloads include large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring massive RAM footprints.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized instance designed for workloads requiring massive memory capacity relative to compute resources. It features 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.2 GHz Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 20 physical cores. The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct trade-off: single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations achieve average, middle-tier results. With approximately 24 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile tailored for memory-bound applications. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, extensive caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads that benefit from a substantial memory footprint rather than high single-threaded processing speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server featuring 40 vCPUs, 961 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 features 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring an exceptionally high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and large-scale database caching.
bullet_points: 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM powered by an Intel Xeon processor
Dedicated x86_64 architecture with 20 physical cores and hyper-threading
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Best suited for large in-memory databases and caching layers
